items.briefing:
Ransomware threats are escalating rapidly, with new research showing AI is helping criminals launch attacks much faster than before — meaning organisations have less time to respond and must prioritise their ability to recover quickly. A ransomware group known as "The Gentlemen" topped the charts in Q2 2026, while a separate group called Inc Ransomware is actively exploiting unpatched vulnerabilities in SonicWall remote access devices. Separately, a Vicarius study found 79% of organisations have been hit by incidents involving known, preventable vulnerabilities.

These findings are a reminder that keeping software and devices up to date is critical — especially internet-facing systems. **Practical tip:** Check with your IT team that any remote access or VPN devices your organisation uses are running the latest firmware and security patches.

featured.title: DigiCert survey finds 81% of Australian organisations hit by AI security incidents or vulnerabilities
featured.quote: AI security incidents already affecting majority of Australian organisations, signalling urgent need for local preparedness.
